subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
Treasure Island includes the following components:

- **1 large game board** depicting a tropical island with a grid overlay
- **1 Long John Silver screen** for hiding the treasure location
- **Miniature figures** for Long John Silver and each pirate player
- **Dry-erase markers** in various colors (one per player plus black for Silver)
- **Search tokens** (small and large radius)
- **Clue cards** used by Long John Silver
- **Direction compass tools** and **ruler/line tools** for drawing clues
- **Calendar track** with day markers
- **Treasure token** (hidden behind Silver's screen)
- **Erasable island map** (the board is dry-erase compatible)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Place the **game board** in the center of the table.
2. One player takes the role of **Long John Silver** and sits behind the screen.
3. Silver **secretly marks the treasure location** on a hidden mini-map behind the screen, choosing any land hex on the island.
4. Set the **calendar marker** to Day 1 on the calendar track.
5. Place all **pirate miniatures** at the starting camp location.
6. Give each pirate player a **dry-erase marker** of their color.
7. Ensure the **drawing tools** (compass, straightedge) are accessible to Silver.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
### Long John Silver
- Choose a treasure location and mark it secretly on the mini-map behind your screen.
- You will give clues each round by drawing on the board with tools.
- You have a limited ability to **mislead** within the rules.

### Pirate Players
- Each pirate starts at the base camp.
- You have a dry-erase marker to make personal notes directly on the board.
- On your turns, you will move your figure and search locations.
- Your goal is to find the treasure before the other pirates — and before Silver escapes.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Treasure Island is a **one-versus-all deduction game**. One player is **Long John Silver**, who has buried treasure somewhere on the island and been captured by the other players (pirates).

Silver is forced to give clues about the treasure's location — but Silver is a crafty pirate who can sometimes **lie or mislead**. The pirate players must interpret the clues, narrow down the search area, and dig in the right spot.

If no pirate finds the treasure before a set number of days pass, **Silver escapes** from captivity and races to retrieve the treasure personally. The game becomes a desperate race between the pirates and Silver.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
The game is played over a series of **days**, each with multiple phases:

### 1. Silver Gives a Clue
At the start of each day, Long John Silver must give **one clue** to all pirates. Clues are physical drawings on the board:
- **Line clue**: Draw a straight line — the treasure is on one side of this line.
- **Circle clue**: Draw a circle around a point — the treasure is NOT within this circle.
- **Directional clue**: From a specific point, indicate a direction — the treasure lies in that general direction.

Silver **must tell the truth** most of the time but has a limited number of **lie tokens** that allow a false clue.

### 2. Pirate Turns
Each pirate takes a turn in clockwise order:
1. **Move**: Move your pirate figure up to **2 spaces** on the hex grid.
2. **Search**: Perform one search action:
   - **Small search**: Check a small radius around your position. Silver says yes/no.
   - **Large search**: Check a larger area but it costs your full action.
   - **Dig**: Attempt to dig at your exact location. If the treasure is there, you win!

### 3. Advance the Calendar
Move the day marker forward. If the calendar reaches the **escape day**, Silver breaks free.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Search Actions (Pirates)
- **Small Search (1 hex)**: Ask Silver if the treasure is at your exact hex. Silver must answer truthfully.
- **Area Search (radius)**: Use a search token to check a circular area. Silver answers yes or no.
- **Dig**: If you believe the treasure is at your current location, declare a dig. If correct, you **win immediately**.

### Clue Drawing (Silver)
Silver uses physical tools to draw clues on the board:
- **Straightedge**: Draw lines to bisect the island. "The treasure is on THIS side."
- **Compass tool**: Draw circles. "The treasure is NOT within this circle."
- These drawings remain on the board, creating an ever-tightening search area.

### Lying (Silver)
Silver has a **limited number of lie tokens** (typically 1–2 depending on player count). When giving a clue, Silver may secretly use a lie token to make that clue **false**. Pirates never know for certain which clues are truthful.

### Silver's Escape
When the calendar reaches the escape day, Silver enters the board as a **moving figure** and races toward the treasure. Silver moves faster than pirates and knows exactly where to go.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Terrain
The island has various terrain types that affect movement:
- **Jungle**: Normal movement cost.
- **Mountains**: Cost extra movement to cross.
- **Water/coast**: Cannot be crossed on foot (must go around).
- **Paths**: Allow faster movement between locations.

### Notebooks and Deduction
Pirates can (and should) use their dry-erase markers to **mark up the board**:
- Cross out eliminated areas.
- Highlight likely zones.
- Note which clues might be lies.

### Information Sharing
Pirates may **discuss clues openly**. However, only one pirate wins — so sharing too much helps your rivals as much as it helps you.

### Forced Clues
Silver **must** give a clue each round. Silver cannot refuse or give an intentionally useless clue (like drawing a line through the ocean). All clues must meaningfully relate to the treasure's position.

### Late Game Urgency
As the escape day approaches, Silver may give increasingly deceptive clues to stall. Pirates must balance thorough deduction with urgency.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
### Pirate Victory
If a pirate **digs at the correct hex** where the treasure is buried, that pirate **wins immediately**. No scoring — it's a direct victory.

### Silver's Escape Victory
If no pirate finds the treasure before the escape day:
1. Silver places their figure on the board at a designated escape point.
2. Silver moves toward the treasure each turn (Silver knows the exact location).
3. Pirates continue searching and can still find the treasure.
4. If **Silver reaches the treasure first**, Silver wins.
5. If a **pirate digs correctly** before Silver arrives, that pirate wins.

### No Treasure Found
If the game reaches its final day with no discovery, Silver wins by default.

### Team Variant
In a cooperative variant, all pirates work together against Silver, sharing all information freely. The pirates collectively win or lose.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Mark the board**: Use your dry-erase marker constantly. Cross out areas eliminated by clues and highlight remaining possibilities.
- **Don't trust everything**: Silver can lie. If a clue seems too helpful, it might be false.
- **Spread out**: Pirates should search different areas of the island rather than clustering together.
- **Area searches first**: Use area searches early to eliminate large zones. Save precise digs for when you're confident.
- **Watch the calendar**: Don't wait too long. As the escape day approaches, start making educated guesses with digs.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
### For Pirates
- **Triangulate**: Combine multiple line clues to create intersection zones. The treasure must be within all truthful clue boundaries.
- **Test for lies**: If you can search an area that contradicts a clue, you'll know if Silver lied — which is hugely valuable information.
- **Don't share everything**: You're competing against other pirates too. Keep some deductions private if you have a strong lead.
- **Cover ground efficiently**: Divide the island into sectors with other pirates. Maximize the area you collectively search.

### For Silver
- **Save lies for critical moments**: Don't waste lie tokens early. Use them when pirates are close to the truth.
- **Give technically true but misleading clues**: A line clue that eliminates only a tiny sliver of ocean is truthful but unhelpful.
- **Misdirect subtly**: If the treasure is in the north, give clues that are true but subtly steer attention south.
- **Plan your escape route**: Know which path you'll take when you break free, and try to keep pirates away from that corridor.
